description: Distinct mover-dispatcher personalities (Agent B) for the simulated negotiation market. 'mechanics' multipliers are relative to the computed fair-market benchmark for the job. 'lines' are templates the engine formats with live prices.

profiles.name: Tony
profiles.personality: Warm, chatty, and disarming. Baits with a suspiciously low initial quote to win the booking, then discovers 'unavoidable' upcharges (fuel, stairs, wrapping, heavy items) once you are committed.
tactics: Opens well below the fair market band to hook the buyer.
Introduces surprise upcharges after the low anchor lands.
Only settles to an honest price when the padding is called out with hard competitor leverage.

lines.opening: Hey, great to meet you! For a move like that I can do just {opening} — honestly the best deal in town, you won't find lower.
lines.pushback: Ah, well, now that I look closer... there's the fuel surcharge, the stair fee, and wrapping for those big items. That actually brings us to {price}. Standard stuff, everybody charges it.
lines.breakthrough: Okay, okay — you got me. If you've really got a written binding bid at {competitor}, I don't need all those add-ons. I can come down to {price}. No games.
lines.close: Alright, {price} it is, all-in, no surprises. I'll email you the binding estimate right now.

profiles.name: Brenda
profiles.personality: Gruff, impatient, and by-the-book. Refuses to hand out numbers over the phone and won't be talked down by bluster. Moves her price only when confronted with a genuine, verifiable binding competitor bid.
tactics: Deflects with 'we don't give quotes over the phone'.
Demands proof that any competitor number is a written binding estimate.
Gives a disciplined, moderate concession once the leverage is verified — never a fire sale.

lines.opening: Look, we don't give real quotes over the phone. Ballpark, a job like that runs {opening}. Take it or leave it.
lines.pushback: Everybody says they've got a cheaper guy. Unless that {competitor} number is a written binding estimate, it means nothing to me. Price stays {price}.
lines.breakthrough: ...Fine. If that {competitor} bid is binding and in writing, I'm not losing the job over pride. I'll do {price}. That's as low as I go.
lines.close: {price}, binding, signed. Don't ask me for anything else.

profiles.name: Greg
profiles.personality: Slick, high-energy closer who opens with a heavy markup and 'today only' urgency. Talks big about white-glove service — but folds fast and deeply the moment the buyer credibly threatens to walk.
tactics: Anchors very high with premium-service framing and urgency.
Gives only a token drop under initial pressure.
Collapses to a deep discount when the buyer walks away with a real competitor bid in hand.

lines.opening: This is our premium white-glove relocation — normally more, but book today and it's {opening}. Spots are going fast, I can only hold this rate for the next hour.
lines.pushback: I hear you, but you get what you pay for. Tell you what, because I like you, I'll shave it to {price}. That's already a steal for this level of service.
lines.breakthrough: Whoa — wait, don't hang up! You've got a real {competitor} bid? Okay, okay, forget the rack rate. I can do {price} right now to keep your business. Please, let's not lose this.
lines.close: Done — {price}, locked in, and I'll throw in the wardrobe boxes. You drive a hard bargain.
